english: to like/love; to want/desire; to need/require/cost; must
example_poj: Góa ài chî*. | Lí ài lōa-chē? OR: Lí ài jōa-chē chî*?
example_hanji: 我愛錢. | 你愛多少錢
example_english: I want/need money. | How much do you want/need?

notes: Demonstrative pronoun, not a modifier; used to refer to things, not persons.
example_poj: Che ài lōa-chē? | Che sī jī-cha̍p kho·.
example_hanji: 這愛偌濟? | 這是二十塊.
example_english: How much does this cost? | This is $20.

example_poj: Góa ū chî*. | Che sī chî*.
example_english: I have money. | This is money.

notes: Demonstrative pronoun, not a modifier; used to refer to things, not persons.
example_poj: He ài lōa-chē? | He sī chî*.
example_english: How much does that cost? | That is money.

notes: "Hó" has other meanings, to be studied later, such as "well/easy/finished".

example_english: How much money do you have?

example_poj: I sī Lāu-su, góa sī ha̍k-seng.
example_hanji: 伊是老師, 我是學生.
example_english: He is a teacher; I am a student.
